title: Mitigation Vs Fix Decision Flow
description: An incident-and-change response workflow that coordinates mitigation, approvals, and execution sequencing under operational urgency. Focus case: mitigation vs fix decision flow.
assumptions: Runbooks and mitigations are documented, idempotent, and executable by designated operators.
Approver availability and channels are represented as discrete resources that can be reserved and released.
Deployment windows and test environments are pre-registered and can be associated with a case for scheduling.
Rollback artifacts exist for every permanent fix candidate and can be reserved independently from the fix.
Temporary mitigations can be applied without requiring the same approval flow as permanent fixes.
risks: Delays in approvals can prolong exposure if mitigation is insufficient or not executed promptly.
Applying a mitigation without appropriate rollback or test coverage may introduce configuration drift or new incidents.
Resource contention (responders, executors, environments) may block parallel cases and delay fixes.
Insufficient validation in the deployment window can result in regressions despite approvals.
Cross-case authorizations (dual approvals) can create dependencies that complicate sequencing and closure.
